item.title: Regulatory Fragmentation
item.description:
DOWNLOAD

We use Federal Register, the daily journal of US regulators to create measure of regulatory fragmentation. The main variable, Regulatory_Fragmentation, reflects the variety of agencies company is exposed to in a given year. Higher values of this variable suggest that a company is regulated by more agencies.

Two additional variables, Topic_Dispersion and Regulation_Quantity, show the dispersion of topics raised by the company in their annual reports and the amount of the relevant regulations. We describe the construction of this data in more details in the paper “Regulatory Fragmentation” with Joseph Kalmenovitz and Michelle Lowry.

Last update: 2020
